steps.title: Use the new point's effect
steps.content: Data set A has best-fit line $y=-5.1+8.7x$, so its slope is $8.7$. The new point is $(9,52)$.

steps.title: Check whether the new point is above or below the old trend
steps.content: Use the old model at $x=9$:$$y=-5.1+8.7(9)=73.2$$The actual new point has $y=52$, which is far below $73.2$. A far-right point below the old trend pulls the best-fit slope down.

steps.title: Check the direction
steps.content: The new slope $s$ should be less than $8.7$. Among the choices, only $5.9$ is less than $8.7$ and reasonable for the downward pull. Choice A is correct.
